title: polypodium phegopteris var. robertianum
intro: Polypodium phegopteris var. robertianum, also known as Polypodium Robertianum, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and even acting as an antihelmintic, which can help to expel intestinal parasites. In herbal preparations, Polypodium phegopteris var. robertianum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or ointment to aid in healing and skin regeneration.
